content: Which of the following is a positively charged sol?
explanation: Methylene blue solution

content: Which one is an example of heterogenous catalysis?

options.content: Hydrolysis of sugar catalysed by $$\mathrm{H}^{+}$$ ions.
Decomposition of ozone is presence of nitrogen monoxide.
Combination between dinitrogen and dihydrogen to form ammonia in the presence of finely divided iron.
Oxidation of sulphur dioxide into sulphur trioxide in the presence of oxides of nitrogen.

explanation:
(1) $$\mathrm{C}_{12} \mathrm{H}_{22} \mathrm{O}_{11 \text { (aq) }}+\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \stackrel{\mathrm{H}^{+}}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{C}_{6} \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_{6(\text { aq) }}+\mathrm{C}_{6} \mathrm{H}_{12} \mathrm{O}_{6(\text { aq )}}$$ (Homogeneous reaction)

(2) $$2 \mathrm{O}_{3(\mathrm{g})} \stackrel{\mathrm{NO}(\mathrm{g})}{\longrightarrow} 3 \mathrm{O}_{2(\mathrm{g})}$$ (Homogeneous reaction)

(3) $$\mathrm{N}_{2(g)}+3 \mathrm{H}_{2(\mathrm{g})} \stackrel{\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{s})}{\longrightarrow} 2 \mathrm{NH}_{3(\mathrm{g)}}$$ (Reactants and catalyst are in different phase) It is heterogeneous reaction

(4) $$\mathrm{SO}_{2(\operatorname{g)}}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{O}_{2(\mathrm{g})} \stackrel{\mathrm{NO}_{(g)}}{\longrightarrow} \mathrm{SO}_{3(\mathrm{g})}$$

explanation: Pumice stone is an example of solid state

content:
Shown below are adsorption isotherms for a gas 'X' at temperatures T1, T2 and T3 :

p and $${x \over m}$$ represent pressure and extent of adsorption, respectively. The correct order of temperatures for the given adsorption is :
explanation: With increase in temperature, extent of adsorption $$\left( {i.e.\,{x \over m}} \right)$$ decreases so correct order of temperatures will be $${T_3} > {T_2} > {T_1}$$

content:
Given below ae two statements

Statement I:

In the coagulation of a negative sol, the flocculating power of the three given ions is in the order Al3+ > Ba2+ > Na+

Statement II:

In the coagulation of a positive sol, the flocculating power of the three given salts is in the order NaCl > Na2SO4 > Na3PO4

In the light of the above statements, choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below

options.content: Both Statement I and Statement II are correct.
Both Statement I and Statement II are incorrect.
Statement I is correct but Statement II is incorrect.
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is correct.

explanation:
According to hardy Schulze rule

$$\bullet$$ Flocculating power of cation increases with increases in charge on cation of electrolyte in case of negatively charge colloid, hence order is

Al3+ > Ba2+ > Na+

$$\bullet$$ Flocculating power of anion increases with increases in charge on anion of electrolyte in case of positively charge colloids

Hence order is

NaCl < Na2SO4 < Na3PO4

content: The right option for the statement "Tyndall effect is exhibited by", is :
explanation:
$$\bullet$$ Tyndall effect is exhibited by colloidal solution only.

$$\bullet$$ Among the given options, Urea, NaCl and Glucose solutions are true solutions, so cannot show Tyndall effect.

$$\bullet$$ Starch solution is a colloidal solution therefore can show Tyndall effect.

content: Measuring Zeta potential is useful in determining which property of colloidal solution?
explanation:
In colloidal solution, the potential difference between the fixed layer and the diffused layer of opposite charge is known as Zeta potential. The presence of equal and similar charges on colloidal particles are largely responsible in providing stability to the colloidal solution.
Zeta potential $$ \propto $$ Stability of colloidal particle

content:
On which of the following properties does the
coagulating power of an ion depend?

options.content:
The magnitude of the charge on the ion
alone

Both magnitude and sign of the charge on
the ion
The sign of charge on the ion alone

explanation:
According to Hardy Schulze rule :
1. Coagulation of colloidal solution by using an electrolyte depends on the charge present (positive or negative) on colloidal particles as well as on its size.
2. Coagulating power of an electrolyte depends on the magnitude of charge present on effective ion of electrolyte.

content: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

options.content: The value of equilibrium constant is changed in the presence of a catalyst in the reaction at equilibrium.
Coenzymes increase the catalytic activity of enzyme.
Catalyst does not initiate any reaction.

explanation:
A catalyst accelerates the forward and backward
reaction to the same extent thus, the equilibrium
constant does not change. Catalyst only speed up
the reactions to attain equilibrium faster and does
not alter equilibrium constant. Equilibrium constant
varies only when temperature condition of reaction
gets changed.

content:
The coagulation values in millimoles per litre of the electrolytes used for the coagulation of As2S3 are given below :
I.  (NaCl) = 52,
II. (BaCl2) = 0.69,
III. (MgSO4) = 0.22
The correct order of their coagulating power is
explanation:
As,

Coagulating power $$\infty $$
1
Coagulation value

Lower the coagulation value, higher is the coagulating power so, the correct order is :

III > II > I

content: Fog is a colloidal solution of
explanation:
Fog is a colloidal system having dispersed
phase as liquid and dispersion medium as gas.

content: Which one of the following characteristics is associated with adsorption?

options.content: $$\Delta $$G and $$\Delta $$H are negative but $$\Delta $$S is positive.
$$\Delta $$G and $$\Delta $$S are negative but $$\Delta $$H is positive.
$$\Delta $$G is negative but $$\Delta $$H and $$\Delta $$S are positive.

explanation:
Adsorption is spontaneous process, therefore change in the free energy ( $$\Delta $$G) for the process is negative.

As we know that

$$\Delta $$G = $$\Delta $$H – T$$\Delta $$S

S is negative because adhering of gas molecules to the surface lowers the randomness.

$$ \therefore $$ $$\Delta $$G can be –ve only when $$\Delta $$H is –ve.

content: Which property of colloidal solution is independent of charge on the colloidal particles?
explanation:
Tyndall effect is the scattering of light by sol
particles which cannot be affected by charge on
them.

content: Which property of colloids is not dependent on the charge on colloidal particles?
explanation:
Tyndall effect is due to the scattering of
light by colloidal particles and not due to the charge.

content: The protecting power of lyophilic colloidal sol is expressed in terms of
explanation:
The lyophobic sols are less stable than lyophilic sols. The lyophilic sols are thus used to protect the lyophobic sols. This property of lyophilic sols is known as protective action of lyophilic sols.

Which can be represented by gold number. Lesser the gold number, higher is the protecting power.

content: Which one of the following statements is incorrect about enzyme catalysis?

options.content: Enzymes are mostly proteinous in nature.
Enzymes are denatured by ultraviolet rays and at high temperature.
enzymes are least reactive at optimum temperature.

explanation:
Enzymes are most reactive at optimum
temperature. The optimum temperature for
enzyme activity lies between 40°C to 60°C.

content: In Freundlich adsorption isotherm, the value of 1/n is
explanation:
Freundlich adsorption isotherm:

$${x \over m} = k.{p^{{1 \over n}}}$$

Where $${x \over m}$$ is the ratio of amount of adsorbent to the amount of adsorbate.

The value of n is always greater than 1. So, the value of $${{1 \over n}}$$ lies between 0 and 1 in all cases.

content: If x is amount of adsorbate and m is amount of adsorbent, which of the following relations is not related to adsorption process?
explanation: $${x \over m} = p \times T$$ is incorrect.

content: The Langmuir adsorption isotherm is deduced using the assumption

options.content: the adsorption sites are equivalent in their ability to adsorb the particles
the heat of adsorption varies with coverage
the adsorbed molecules interact with each other
the adsorption takes place in multilayers.

explanation:
Langmuir adsorption isotherm is based
on the assumption that every adsorption site is
equivalent and that the ability of a particle to
bind there is independent of whether nearby sites
are occupied or not occupied.

content: A plot of log(x/m) versus log p for the adsorption of a gas on a solid gives a straight line with slope equal to
explanation:
According to Freundlich adsorption isotherm, in the median range of pressure

       $${x \over m} \propto {P^{{1 \over n}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow $$  $${x \over m}$$ = kP$$^{{1 \over n}}$$

taking log both sides, we get,

log$${x \over m}$$ = logk + $${1 \over n}$$ logP

Here in graph between log$${x \over m}$$ and logP, slope is $${1 \over n}$$ and intercepts = log k.

content: Which one of the following forms micelles in aqueous solution above certain concentration?
explanation: Dodecyl trimethyl ammonium chloride is a cationic surfactant and forms micelles in aqueous solution above critical micelle concentration (CMC).

content: The enzyme which hydrolyses triglycerides to fatty acids and glycerol is called
explanation:
Triglycerides are lipids, hence these are
hydrolysed by lipases to glycerol and fatty acids.

content: According to the adsorption theory of catalysis, the speed of the reaction increases because

options.content: the concentration of reactant molecules at the active centres of the catalyst becomes high due to adsorption
in the process of adsorption , the activation energy of the molecules becomes large
adsorption produces heat which increases the speed of the reaction
adsorption lowers the activation energy of the reaction.

explanation:
The speed of the reaction increases because
adsorption lowers the activation energy of the
reaction.

content: Position of non polar and polar part in micelle

options.content: polar at outer surface but non polar at inner surface
polar at inner surface non polar at outer surface
are present in the surface only.

explanation:
Micelles are formed by the association of colloids.
They are formed by lyophilic and lyophobic groups.
As the concentration increases the lyophobic parts
receding away from the solvent approach each
other are forms a cluster, the lyophobic ends are in
the interior, lyophilic groups projecting outwards in
contact with the solvent.

content: Which is not correct regarding the adsorption of a gas on surface of a solid?

options.content: On increasing temperature adsorption increases continuously.
Enthalpy and entropy change is negative.
Adsorption is more for some specific substance.

explanation:
Adsorption is the property of a substance to
hold or to concentrate liquid or dissolved substances
upon its surface. Solids adsorb greater amount of
substance at lower temperature and in general
adsorption decreases with increasing temperature.

content: Which one of the following method is commonly used method for destruction of colloid?
explanation:
By adding electrolytes the colloidal
particles are precipitated. The electrolytes neutralise
the charge of colloids leading to their coagulation
and thus destroy the colloid.

content: Which mixture of the solution will lead to the formation of negatively charged colloidal [AgI] I<sup>–</sup> sol. ?

options.content: 50 mL of 2 M AgNO<sub>3</sub> + 50 mL of 1.5 M KI
50 mL of 0.1 M AgNO<sub>3</sub> + 50 mL of 0.1 M KI
50 mL of 1 M AgNO<sub>3</sub> + 50 mL of 1.5 M KI
50 mL of 1 M AgNO<sub>3</sub> + 50 mL of 2 M KI

explanation:
AgNO3 + KI $$ \to $$ AgI + KNO3

If KI is in excess then negatively charged colloid, [AgI]I– is formed. Whereas, if AgNO3 is in excess positively charged colloid AgI/Ag+ will form.
